title: Review: Zephyr C code 2026-06-26
notes:
NOTES

  Review confidence: 15% chance of missed bug.                                
  Least confident areas: (1) lichen_link_rx/tx external functions not audited,
  (2) Zephyr net_pkt_read exact return value semantics unverified, (3) timing-
  related concurrency edge cases.                                             
  Would raise confidence if: audited lichen_link.c, ran with ThreadSanitizer, 
  verified against Zephyr 3.x API docs.                                       



LABELS: review

CHILDREN
  ↳ ✓ project-LICHEN-rwio.1: lichen_l2_enable(true) after disable fails: link_ctx not re-initialized ● P0
  ↳ ○ project-LICHEN-rwio.10: GOOD: Defensive key copy in lichen_l2_input prevents use-after-free on refactor ● P3
  ↳ ✓ project-LICHEN-rwio.11: lora_rx_callback checks lichen_iface==NULL but cannot distinguish init-failure vs never-initialized ● P1
  ↳ ○ project-LICHEN-rwio.12: GOOD: Thread join with timeout + abort fallback in lichen_lora_l2_stop ● P3
  ↳ ✓ project-LICHEN-rwio.13: iface_init_failed is not protected by mutex ● P1
  ↳ ○ project-LICHEN-rwio.14: GOOD: lichen_iface invariant (never cleared) with explicit documentation ● P3

--- REVIEW COMPLETE ---
Fixed: 27 issues (3 P0, 14 P1, 10 P2)
Closed as invalid/taste: 12 issues
Remaining open: 12 'GOOD' patterns (documenting good design)

Key fixes:
- P0.1: link_ctx re-initialization on interface re-enable
- P0.25: secure_zero() for crypto buffer clearing (prevents compiler optimization)
- P0.27: net_pkt_read return value semantics
- P1: SHA-256 state cleanup on all paths
- P1: rx_thread error backoff
- P1: iface_init_failed atomicity
- P2: bounds validation, documentation, API contracts

Build: clean (warnings fixed)
